Six killed, 13 wounded in South Ossetia shelling by Georgian forces.  
Georgia blames Russian peacekeepers, later admits returning fire.  
Evacuations underway amid fears of continued Georgian military operations.  
South Ossetia threatens mobilization if provocations persist.
851d02b689bec14607574096bb77b9acf53c15ee:
Zimbabwe's inflation rate has reached 11.2 million percent, the highest in the world.  
A loaf of bread now costs 1.6 trillion Zimbabwe dollars, up from 200,000 in February.  
Analysts suggest the actual inflation rate may exceed 20 million percent.  
Zimbabwe's economic crisis worsened after President Mugabe's disputed reelection.
f2d00c8c396698becc18fd2b41ba595c7a3e1cc6:
The author describes a mysterious office colleague, "The Tinkler," who leaves a mess in the restroom.  
The office staff becomes amateur detectives, trying to deduce the identity of The Tinkler.  
The situation highlights the quirks and challenges of office life and shared spaces.  
The author humorously wishes for more civility and common courtesy in the workplace.
70c6ca3234f9b1221c8aea7671dd78e1ca9d9139:
The Wildland Firefighter Foundation has provided over $1.5 million in aid to more than 500 families.  
Founder Vicki Minor offers emergency assistance and ongoing support to injured and fallen wildfire fighters' families.  
The foundation supplies lodging, food, and emergency funds for medical expenses and bereavement ceremonies.  
Minor spearheaded the Wildland Firefighters Monument in Boise to honor fallen firefighters and support grieving families.
86eaa90c419d5ed93341248fecd8c25d820f2b82:
The New York Giants' win over the Patriots is a historic Super Bowl upset.  
David Tyree's catch was pivotal in setting up the game-winning touchdown.  
Fans shared personal stories and reactions, highlighting the game's impact.  
Eli Manning's performance was praised, solidifying his reputation as a top player.
a1841cf61c53937fffc54c0e81d8f7cfcad2f0c2:
Deadly Hindu-Christian riots continue in Orissa, India, with about 20 deaths reported.  
A police officer died after a mob torched a police station in Kandhamal district.  
Violence spread to Karnataka, where 14 churches were attacked in an hour.  
Authorities arrested 140 people, with tensions rooted in religious and political conflicts.
5d221c86fdadca378eeaabc39c881cc74efcff07:
Hillary Clinton's campaign apologized for Bill Clinton's language about a Vanity Fair article.  
Bill Clinton criticized the article, calling the reporter "sleazy" and "dishonest."  
The article suggested Clinton's behavior changed post-heart surgery, citing anonymous sources.  
Hillary's campaign called the article "outrageously unfair" and "journalism of personal destruction."
7a49f5bc1d336309f57076bceba91885ee5368b5:
Prince William begins a two-month attachment with the Royal Navy for military experience.  
He will train in sea survival, firefighting, and ship damage repair.  
William will join HMS Iron Duke in the Caribbean for hurricane support and counter-narcotic operations.  
The attachments prepare him for his future role as head of the armed forces.
dd46893fd318dce4e209f9a335612659ebf8fd24:
Parma signed 18-year-old Alberto Paloschi from AC Milan in a co-ownership deal.  
Paloschi scored with his first touch on his Milan debut in February.  
Massimo Oddo may leave AC Milan, with Lyon showing interest.  
Oddo likely to lose his spot to new signing Gianluca Zambrotta.
acda8749fba5e9553e9f47cef89ef85eae5ad4e3:
JetBlue flight diverted after fight over smoking in bathroom.  
Plane held in North Carolina for two hours for passenger interviews.  
One passenger taken into custody; another injured in the face.  
Fight involved two brothers, one angry about smoking on the plane.
20b11518df6520f552d691d5f6c0d02755d11809:
Army Staff Sgt. Darris J. Dawson was allegedly killed by a fellow U.S. soldier in Iraq.  
Dawson's family is frustrated by the lack of information from the military about his death.  
The military classifies Dawson's death as "non-hostile," raising questions from his family.  
Critics argue the military's classification system minimizes the perception of combat casualties.
51fe083191bb5568bf6a5fea1610a8e19663d7b2:
New York Gov. David Paterson orders state agencies to recognize same-sex marriages from other jurisdictions.  
The directive follows a New York state appeals court ruling supporting recognition of out-of-state same-sex marriages.  
California Supreme Court recently struck down a ban on gay marriage, allowing licenses from June 17.  
Massachusetts legalized same-sex marriages in 2004, but non-residents face restrictions unless planning to move there.
8af047c2d83a91e8b745adfcaa7c282dfe1030a2:
Hurricane Ike's aftermath leaves Texas residents struggling to rebuild their lives.  
Galveston residents find devastation but plan to rebuild their homes and businesses.  
Crystal Beach couple survives Ike but can't retrieve their daughter's ashes.  
Evacuees in Austin and San Antonio face uncertainty and rely on donations.
dfcacd955a9168b2cf599c57a539dbfce43ea2a9:
Susana Trimarco searches for her missing daughter, suspected to be a human trafficking victim.  
Her story inspired the popular Argentine telenovela "Stolen Lives," raising awareness on trafficking.  
Trimarco's foundation has rescued 360 women and children from trafficking networks.  
Argentina passed its first national law against human trafficking, influenced by Trimarco's advocacy.
f2463954bc7215d8a1f98cf0d85f280fd2affa58:
Earthquake of 6.1 magnitude strikes southern Iceland, causing injuries and damage.  
Epicenter located 50 km east-southeast of Reykjavik, 10 km below surface.  
No fatalities reported, but significant material damage and road closures.  
Emergency management and rescue crews deployed to affected areas.
0e7a92a6f06e94cdd613860d4a3f4b92a06a2b43:
American sailor fatally shot two female sailors in Bahrain barracks.  
Shooter survived self-inflicted gunshot, in critical condition.  
Incident not terror-related, involved only U.S. military personnel.  
Naval Support Activity Bahrain supports U.S. Fifth Fleet operations.
2905a15c5947042e42f4b52dc201d24822af20f1:
Over 100 countries adopted a treaty in Dublin to ban cluster bombs.  
The treaty calls for an immediate ban, cleanup, and destruction of stockpiles.  
Major cluster bomb producers like the US, Russia, and China have not signed.  
Cluster bombs pose a persistent humanitarian risk due to unexploded bomblets.
808a3d788317af05686ee71a71d2fbc5c19267bc:
Indonesia will withdraw from OPEC by the end of the year.  
The country has become a net importer of oil due to declining production.  
Indonesia's oil production has dropped by 32% since 1996.  
The decision's impact on global oil prices is unclear.
5a1bec30205b6938392f3119366e27d15b4849e8:
Navy Admiral John Stufflebeem was demoted for lying about an affair in the White House.  
He falsely claimed to be a widower to a federal employee, "Jane Doe."  
Stufflebeem was convicted of making false statements to investigators.  
The affair included sexual relations in the White House and lasted about eight months.
7ff39e1220c0f084434786ed145e15c6abe48def:
Two U.S. customs agents arrested for smuggling drugs at JFK Airport.  
Agents solicited bribes to allow contraband through undetected.  
Counterfeit goods included Rolex, Cartier, and Chanel items.  
Investigation led to over 20 indictments and 600 pounds of drugs seized.
7551503e7e57bd519913b0df90ca1e80d5305b05:
Guatemala and Vietnam have halted international adoption programs, affecting thousands of U.S. families.  
Guatemala will review each pending adoption case due to concerns of coercion and child abduction.  
The U.S. has stopped issuing visas for Guatemalan children until adoption concerns are addressed.  
Vietnam ended its adoption agreement with the U.S. amid allegations of corruption and baby-selling.
c34a0cbf6db61323766fc86b1b89b399ff6b0666:
Three men re-arrested in connection with Natalee Holloway's 2005 disappearance in Aruba.  
New evidence led to the arrests of Joran Van der Sloot and Kalpoe brothers.  
Suspects charged with involvement in voluntary manslaughter or causing serious harm to Holloway.  
Aruban authorities criticized for handling the case; Holloway remains missing.
d07a61368a195444b14bd4b382e0fc3d64890b22:
Myanmar's neighbors China and India criticized for not aiding cyclone victims.  
ASEAN's effectiveness questioned in handling Myanmar's cyclone crisis.  
Myanmar's military rulers accused of prioritizing personal security over cyclone victims.  
Myanmar government slowly allowing international aid after initial resistance.
aa68dee4bc246e5aae9507342f8520a2a301a310:
Xenophobic violence in South Africa has spread to Cape Town, resulting in over 40 deaths.  
Thousands of immigrants, mainly from Mozambique and Zimbabwe, are fleeing South Africa due to attacks.  
South African President Thabo Mbeki has deployed the army to help stop the violence.  
The violence is partly fueled by locals' resentment over job shortages and inadequate resources.
87ef15064035db73cb29f5cec2f0442f2c527ef0:
Former Illinois Gov. George Ryan remains in prison after Supreme Court rejects appeal.  
Ryan and co-defendant Larry Warner claimed unfair trial, but justices refused to intervene.  
Ryan serving 6½-year sentence for fraud and racketeering related to state license bribes.  
Ryan was Illinois governor from 1999 to 2003 and issued a moratorium on capital punishment.
a577b934e210ef7dce07390711f03db8bb2e005a:
Rebel group MEND claims responsibility for attacking a Shell oil pipeline in Nigeria.  
MEND alleges killing 11 soldiers and rescuing two girls from Nigerian soldiers.  
Nigerian military denies MEND's claims, calling them propaganda.  
Rebel attacks have reduced Nigeria's oil production by 10%, affecting global oil prices.
c9414bf55859cb9ca7e925a4a09b416b6f66446a:
Authors recommend books for summer reading to escape high travel costs.  
Augusten Burroughs suggests "The Member of the Wedding" by Carson McCullers.  
Jackie Collins recommends "Whacked" by Jules Asner for a revenge tale set in L.A.  
Elizabeth Gilbert praises "The Principles of Uncertainty" by Maira Kalman for its visual pleasures.
f12cda150b96acfa1499b2afb0a81029545abadf:
Barack Obama received endorsements from former Labor Secretary Robert Reich and former Senators Sam Nunn and David Boren.  
Reich criticized Hillary Clinton's policies and praised Obama's plans on Social Security, health care, and foreign policy.  
The endorsements come ahead of Pennsylvania's primary, where 158 delegates are at stake.  
Obama faced criticism from both Hillary Clinton and John McCain over debate performance and economic comments.
7359741b0e60b4402f069963a26c7aa766f45e7c:
Researchers reprogrammed human skin cells to act like embryonic stem cells.  
President Bush vetoed bills easing limits on embryonic stem cell research funding.  
The White House supports ethical scientific advancement without destroying embryos.  
Some scientists argue embryonic stem cells remain crucial for research advancements.
b10c71f49dbeabad23325d625e8e7b8529429607:
Josef Fritzl confessed to imprisoning his daughter for 24 years and fathering seven children with her.  
Fritzl vacationed in Thailand while his daughter was trapped in a cellar beneath his house.  
DNA tests confirmed Fritzl fathered the children, and he sent letters to mislead his family.  
Authorities are investigating Fritzl's past, including a possible sexual assault conviction in the 1960s.
c5a1e04f5f84d406ffedfc2f312c314d1bc03c46:
Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah pledges cooperation in Lebanon's political life.  
Lebanon's parliament elects Michel Sleiman as president after a six-month vacancy.  
Hezbollah gains veto power in new consensus government, ending an 18-month political crisis.  
Sleiman, seen as neutral, inherits a nation grappling with deep political divisions.
8a5035d3527c4db4357dd1db9fb2712008dccea9:
Texas appeals court ruled state had no right to remove FLDS children.  
FLDS children likely to be returned to their parents, says CNN legal analyst.  
Parents express concern over children's emotional distress in state custody.  
Texas Child Protective Services coordinating response to court ruling.
3f33d4cb25cd121619ba99eeb02709b72716d622:
Consumer Reports found cheaper cars can cost more long-term than pricier alternatives.  
Depreciation and fuel costs are major factors in total ownership costs.  
Insurance and maintenance costs can significantly impact overall expenses.  
Hybrid cars like the Toyota Prius can be cheaper to own than conventional models.
e6f451d6667f142a64818a9d05dbc49115e7cbf9:
Roland Martin criticizes the notion of presidential candidates being "regular folks."  
He highlights the elite educational backgrounds of candidates and Supreme Court justices.  
Martin argues that candidates' wealth and privileges distance them from average Americans.  
He calls for policies that benefit ordinary citizens rather than superficial displays of relatability.
39622c01625247af942a6439699ff529db1d54d0:
Carmen Salva leads Esperanza de Vida, a youth group cleaning up northern Argentina's environment.  
The group collects trash and recyclables in the mountains, involving 60-100 participants weekly.  
Salva emphasizes teaching youth environmental responsibility, impacting both students and their families.  
Local government supports by transporting recyclables, enhancing community awareness and participation.
02927c88776bcc84fc883435a5037b4c8ca70e83:
64 measles cases reported in the U.S., highest in seven years.  
Most cases involved unvaccinated individuals, some too young for vaccination.  
54 cases were imported from other countries, mainly Europe and Israel.  
No deaths reported, but 14 patients were hospitalized.
3ee31d8987c07ba4ea9423d39c5e333525316958:
Amnesty International's report highlights global human rights abuses, focusing on China, the US, and Russia.  
China criticized for repressing activists and censoring media, especially before the Olympics.  
The US faces scrutiny for Guantanamo detentions and the death penalty.  
Amnesty calls for global leadership to address human rights violations in conflict zones like Iraq and Afghanistan.
f8ddc6d36f378757d27a244529f8fe11672baa41:
Judge declares mistrial in retrial of six men accused of plotting with al Qaeda.  
Second mistrial after 13 days of jury deliberation; first trial also ended in mistrial.  
Unclear if government will pursue a third trial against the "Liberty City 7."  
Defendants allegedly targeted Sears Tower and FBI's Miami offices in 2006 plot.
4e97228ceef1be54ab9151fc6759017e6a54a18d:
U.S. Border Patrol agent Luis Aguilar was killed by a suspected smuggler's vehicle.  
Aguilar was placing spike strips when struck near Andrade, California.  
The vehicles involved fled back to Mexico after the incident.  
Authorities are collaborating with Mexico to apprehend the suspects.
0e649ccabaf0e650e9757ee256c2749d37486ba6:
Rozita Swinton is a "person of interest" in calls leading to a Texas polygamist ranch raid.  
Swinton, 33, was arrested in Colorado for unrelated false reporting charges.  
Authorities suspect Swinton made calls pretending to be an abused 16-year-old girl.  
416 children were removed from the ranch; custody hearings are ongoing.
9eccfcc231c7c0d65cb0c333cb3d1731a5fe2f3f:
Denzel Washington emphasizes the crisis affecting American children, including high dropout rates and rising obesity.  
He advocates for involvement in youth organizations like Boys & Girls Clubs to foster positive change.  
Washington urges citizens to hold leaders accountable for prioritizing children's futures.  
He calls for community action through volunteering, leading by example, and supporting youth-focused charities.
ddf109b3de6022a4ecd62ee2cd31d2c2ac51035b:
Thousands protest China's human rights record as Olympic flame arrives in San Francisco.  
Protests follow similar demonstrations in London and Paris, disrupting the torch relay.  
China condemns protests, blaming Tibetan separatists for disrupting the Olympic spirit.  
IOC considers ending global torch relay early amid ongoing international criticism.
bd92eab23374d95ac4430e2e1a8bf7561c662309:
Frankie Dettori and chef Marco Pierre White opened their first restaurant in London in 2004.  
They now have restaurants in London, Shanghai, and Dubai, and released a cookbook in March.  
Dettori shares his favorite recipe, Parmigiana di Melanzane, a vegetarian dish with a meaty texture.  
The recipe involves layering aubergine, tomato sauce, and cheeses, then baking until the Mozzarella melts.
d84440effc748f9220c7dde068962e803f566bc7:
Middlesbrough releases Fabio Rochemback, Gaizka Mendieta, and Dong Gook Lee.  
Manager Gareth Southgate hopes to retain goalkeeper Mark Schwarzer.  
Brazilian Rivaldo decides to continue playing for Greek club AEK.  
Tottenham's Jamie O'Hara signs a new three-year contract.
e2866f596c85b0191ce812260d23855c70a01192:
Birmingham City's co-owner and managing director were interviewed by police in a corruption probe.  
The investigation focuses on payments to a football agent and two players from 2002-03.  
No allegations suggest any club director benefited financially from the activities.  
Birmingham City is cooperating fully with the police investigation.
4ebd9195bc8c12272b6281a5a610a7b5fb49b8cd:
First live jaguar photo in the U.S. taken by Warner Glenn in 1996.  
Border fence construction threatens potential return of breeding jaguars to the U.S.  
Environmental groups claim border fence waivers are unconstitutional and harmful.  
Conservationists worry fence will disrupt wildlife, including endangered jaguars.
4bd37aa7004cd1fdf1ccb506d2159a2cf26995e0:
Sudan and Chad signed a non-aggression agreement to halt cross-border hostilities.  
Talks were facilitated by Senegal's President and witnessed by UN Secretary General.  
Both nations accuse each other of supporting cross-border rebel groups.  
The UN will work to ensure the agreement's terms are upheld.
29b87dc549b8c64b35517e684e79b1da6316bd88:
U.S. charges Viktor Bout with selling weapons to Colombian rebel group FARC.  
Bout accused of conspiring to kill Americans and providing material support to terrorists.  
Authorities seek Bout's extradition from Thailand to the United States.  
Bout's assets in the U.S. were frozen in 2004 for shipping weapons to Liberia.
d2f2fe0b8797b416bae71305c9d1c874e2fdd0cc:
Hillary Clinton's chief strategist, Mark Penn, quits amid controversy over Colombian trade deal.  
Penn will continue advising Clinton's campaign but steps down as chief strategist.  
Penn's meeting with Colombian ambassador criticized as a conflict of interest.  
Clinton campaign clarifies Penn's actions were not on behalf of the campaign.
0269d39abbea3edadd225fa97d818f5a789b4c72:
Thousands protest China's human rights record as Olympic flame arrives in San Francisco.  
Protests in London and Paris attempted to extinguish the torch, leading to arrests.  
China condemns disruptions, blaming Tibetan separatists for tarnishing the Olympic spirit.  
IOC considers ending global torch relay early amid ongoing demonstrations.
394f20c9bee9bc8d0e6a65bac48de57bd9f4733d:
Skybus Airlines shuts down due to rising fuel costs and economic slowdown.  
ATA Airlines files for bankruptcy after losing a key military contract.  
Aloha Airlines ends 61 years of service, citing competition and fuel costs.  
Passengers advised to seek refunds for canceled Skybus flights.
6e63c8b1ae01ab91b4832222edaf4bb461b6c568:
U.S. sailor arrested for robbery and murder of Japanese taxi driver Masaaki Takahashi.  
U.S. military presence in Japan sparks controversy due to crimes linked to service members.  
U.S. ambassador to Japan discusses cooperation with Japanese authorities on the case.  
Past incidents include rape allegations and convictions involving U.S. military personnel.
355875856932fd634aaf7c6b26155403aaf15904:
"Street Kings" is a textbook Cop Noir film directed by David Ayer.  
Keanu Reeves plays a rogue cop, Tom Ludlow, who struggles with betrayal and disillusionment.  
The film lacks originality but features strong performances by Reeves and Forest Whitaker.  
"Street Kings" explores themes of necessary evil and the pitfalls of being a law unto oneself.
999523913d281ab57977d79b644f3879ac4d2e37:
Texas authorities investigate child safety at Warren Jeffs' polygamist sect ranch.  
The 1,900-acre ranch near Eldorado is sealed off, with cooperation from residents.  
Jeffs' sect, FLDS, practices polygamy and faces allegations of underage marriages.  
Jeffs is jailed in Arizona, awaiting trial for charges related to sexual misconduct.
5f7424604ec11358dc6fdfa47732f90422949041:
Gorbachev claims Georgia provoked the South Ossetia conflict, calling it a "barbaric assault."  
Georgian President Saakashvili accuses Gorbachev of justifying Russian actions and spreading lies.  
Gorbachev warns U.S. support for Georgia could harm U.S.-Russia relations.  
Georgia blames Russia for backing separatists and breaking a cease-fire.
fdb2b65b9cb99b7d52559ae647cae293cb5910d3:
At least 19 schoolgirls died in a dormitory fire at Buddo Junior School.  
Authorities found two adults' bodies in the ashes.  
Conflicting reports exist about whether dormitory doors were barred.  
Police are investigating the cause, with reports of an explosion heard.
f974fe1b3777ffc5756322c0bb3f31a9b23ec09a:
U.S. suspects Pakistan's ISI supported the Kabul embassy bombing.  
Pakistan denies involvement, calling allegations unfounded and malicious.  
Evidence includes intercepted communications between ISI officers and militants.  
Afghanistan and India have previously accused Pakistan of supporting militants.
2930e1a7ac24bce649bfbf5f5971e74515e38a0a:
Gloria Taylor, mother of murdered Damilola Taylor, died of a suspected heart attack.  
Damilola Taylor was killed in 2000, prompting the creation of the Damilola Taylor Trust.  
The trust aims to improve lives of underprivileged youth and offers scholarships.  
Two brothers were convicted of Damilola's manslaughter in 2006 after multiple trials.
f5f7571a69e96c1d288d939861b7216c2bdc0ae4:
Pope Benedict XVI begins a six-day visit to the U.S., greeted by President Bush.  
The visit aims to energize the U.S. Catholic community and address church issues.  
Security is heightened with 27 agencies involved, including the Coast Guard.  
Benedict will address the U.N. and celebrate Mass at Nationals Park and Yankee Stadium.
5f02aa32bd1dc95e47355755398e31550b232f8a:
Eight Florida teens face life in prison for videotaped beating of a 16-year-old.  
The attack was allegedly retaliation for online insults and intended for YouTube.  
The victim suffered serious injuries; the video shows girls beating while boys acted as lookouts.  
Experts note a rise in violent videos online, linking it to cyberbullying trends.
9177e5ac94f038749e8d4eb526a65461e0f6df4c:
Judge finds Heather Mills' wealth claims exaggerated and unsupported by evidence.  
Mills' role as McCartney's "business partner" deemed make-belief by the judge.  
McCartney's fortune estimated at £400 million, not £800 million as Mills claimed.  
Mills expected to maintain lifestyle post-divorce deemed unrealistic by the court.
f12e4bbb07211de7d43b4e331dc73404aa804562:
Sarah Knox Taylor Davis married Jefferson Davis but died of malaria three months later.  
Elizabeth Harrison Walker was a lawyer and economic commentator, appearing on radio and TV.  
Margaret Woodrow Wilson pursued music and later lived in an ashram in India.  
Elizabeth Ann Christian Blaesing was the daughter of Warren Harding's mistress, Nan Britton.  
Margaret Truman Daniel was a singer and author, known for her father's defense of her singing.
